question: What is the fundamental assumption behind the Naive Bayes classifier when applied to text?
options: Features (words) are conditionally independent given the class label.
The order of words in a document is critically important.
All words are equally frequent across all classes.
It uses deep neural networks to model context.
explanation: The 'naive' in Naive Bayes refers to the simplifying assumption that the features (words) are independent of each other given the class label. This makes the probability calculations computationally feasible.
